alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gestural input is a more suitable mode of interaction during driving compared to traditional touch-based and voice command systems. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gestural input is a more suitable mode of interaction during driving compared to traditional touch-based and voice command systems. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion with essentially identical wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current methodologies for object referencing in driving scenarios show a limited capacity to adapt to individual drivers' behaviors and varying driving conditions. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation about current methodologies' limited adaptability appears in the preprint but is not reflected in the publication's claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: IcRegress, a novel regression-based incremental learning approach, effectively adapts to changing driver behavior and characteristics during the dual task of driving and referencing objects. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed IcRegress approach demonstrates superior performance in adapting to individual drivers' behaviors compared to single-instance trained models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the claim as a comparative performance statement (superiority vs single-instance models), which clarifies and narrows the original adaptation claim into a relative performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The incremental learning models demonstrated superiority over single trained models across various driver traits and driving conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evaluation of the approach highlighted its superiority across various driver traits and driving conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the evaluation result with the same scope and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach is available as an open-source framework to facilitate reproducibility and further research. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework is available as an open-source resource to facilitate reproducibility and further research. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same open-source claim but expresses it with higher certainty, representing amplified confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: IcRegress employs continuous lifelong learning to enhance driver experience, safety, and convenience. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This claim emphasizing continuous lifelong learning and explicit benefits (experience, safety, convenience) does not appear in the preprint and is newly introduced in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed flexible parallel neural network (FPNN) model achieves outstanding predictive accuracy in early prediction of battery life (EPBL) tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FPNN provides a precise, adaptable, and easy-to-understand solution for early prediction of End of Life (EPEOL) in lithium batteries. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes precision, adaptability, and interpretability rather than the original blanket statement of 'outstanding predictive accuracy', and it does so with lower expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The FPNN model demonstrates a mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) of 0.88% when the input cyclic data volume is 40. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FPNN achieves MAPE values as low as 0.22% across various cycle data on the MIT dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric performance claim has changed substantially (0.88% → 0.22%) and was broadened to cover various cycle data on a specific dataset; the publication presents the result with high certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The FPNN can adaptively adjust the number of InceptionBlocks to flexibly handle tasks of varying complexity in EPBL. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state an explicit claim about adaptively adjusting the number of InceptionBlocks; that specific adaptability claim is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The interpretability of the FPNN is enhanced by its flexible unit structure and diverse branching structure, which allows it to capture features at different scales. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The diverse branching structure of the flexible units in FPNN allows the model to capture features at different scales. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion about branching structure enabling multi-scale feature capture and interpretability.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Flexible Parallel Neural Network (FPNN) effectively extracts electrochemical features from video-format data through the 3D CNN. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about using a 3D CNN to extract electrochemical features from video-format data is present in the publication but not in the preprint, so it is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Synthetic training samples with non-fluent target sentences can improve translation performance when used in a multilingual machine translation framework. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Synthetic training samples with non-fluent target sentences can improve translation performance when used in a multilingual machine translation framework. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same experimental result and claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach consistently improves translation performance compared to state-of-the-art methods for generating synthetic training samples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach consistently improves translation performance compared to state-of-the-art methods for generating synthetic training samples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The comparative improvement claim is retained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The improvement in translation performance is independent of the size of the original training corpus. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The improvement in translation performance is independent of the size of the original training corpus. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same conclusion about corpus-size independence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The resulting systems are much more robust against domain shift and produce less hallucinations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The resulting systems are much more robust against domain shift and produce less hallucinations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim about robustness to domain shift and reduced hallucinations is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A multi-modal method for predicting trailerness assists editors in selecting trailer-worthy moments from long-form videos. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A multi-modal method for predicting trailerness assists editors in selecting trailer-worthy moments from long-form videos. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predicting trailerness is a challenging task that benefits from multi-modal information. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predicting trailerness is a challenging task that benefits from multi-modal information. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Results are presented on a newly introduced soap opera dataset. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Results are presented on a newly introduced soap opera dataset. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The dataset and presented results are preserved in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A dataset containing text-based user interactions related to behaviour change with two GPT-4-based conversational agents has been shared. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset includes conversation data, user language analysis, perception measures, and user feedback for LLM-generated turns. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset includes conversation data, user language analysis, perception measures, and user feedback for LLM-generated turns. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset can offer valuable insights to inform the design of conversational agents based on real interactions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset can offer valuable insights to inform the design of conversational agents based on real interactions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The refined model can rapidly generate accurate 3D reconstructions of the entire lumbar spine from as few as three intraoperative fluoroscopic shots. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The enhanced model achieved an 84% F1 score, equating to the benchmark set solely by synthetic data in previous research. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach provides real-time capabilities with a computational time of only 81.1 ms, essential for surgery integration. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach offers real-time capabilities with a computational time of just 81.1 ms, vital for successful integration into active surgical procedures. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study devised a novel data collection protocol for a paired dataset consisting of synthetic and real fluoroscopic images from the same perspectives. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The research marks a significant step forward in intraoperative 3D reconstruction, offering enhancements to surgical planning, navigation, and robotics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This innovation has the potential to enhance intraoperative surgical planning, navigation, and surgical robotics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the impact more cautiously as "potential to enhance" rather than asserting it "offers" enhancements, reducing the strength of the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach can effectively recover meaningful demonstrations and show human-like behavior of an agent in the Minecraft environment in a wide variety of scenarios. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach can effectively recover meaningful demonstrations and show human-like behavior of an agent in the Minecraft environment in a wide variety of scenarios. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results reveal that performance of our search-based approach clearly wins in terms of accuracy and perceptual evaluation over learning-based models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results reveal that performance of our search-based approach clearly wins in terms of accuracy and perceptual evaluation over learning-based models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental superiority claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose to use latent spaces of pre-trained foundation models to index a demonstration dataset and instantly access similar relevant experiences. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Pseudo-healthy reconstruction for unsupervised anomaly detection does not require pixel-wise data annotation and can generalize to various anomalies, including rare diseases. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed evaluation procedure simulates realistic abnormal images to validate pseudo-healthy reconstruction methods in the absence of ground truth. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of this framework to 3D brain FDG PET reconstruction aims to detect neurodegeneration markers specific to dementia, such as Alzheimer's disease, as early as possible. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of this framework to 3D brain FDG PET reconstruction aims to detect early neurodegeneration markers specific to dementia, such as Alzheimer’s disease. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework can serve as a preliminary automatic step to validate the capacity of a generative model to reconstruct pseudo-healthy images. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: cDVGAN provides synthetic data that better captures the features of the original data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The conditional derivative GAN (cDVGAN) generates synthetic data that better captures the features of original gravitational wave and glitch data compared to baseline GAN models. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our proposed cDVGAN outperforms 4 different baseline GAN models in replicating the features of the three classes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The conditional derivative GAN (cDVGAN) generates synthetic data that better captures the features of original gravitational wave and glitch data compared to baseline GAN models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the outperforming claim to "baseline GAN models" without the preprint's specific "4 models / three classes" detail, reducing specificity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Training convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with our cDVGAN-generated data improves the detection of samples embedded in detector noise beyond the synthetic data from other state-of-the-art GAN models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Training convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with cDVGAN-generated data improves detection performance in the presence of detector noise, yielding up to a 4.2% increase in area-under-the-curve (AUC) performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the improvement claim and adds a quantified AUC benefit, clarifying the magnitude of the effect.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our best synthetic dataset yields as much as a 4.2% increase in area-under-the-curve (AUC) performance compared to synthetic datasets from baseline GANs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Training convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with cDVGAN-generated data improves detection performance in the presence of detector noise, yielding up to a 4.2% increase in area-under-the-curve (AUC) performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the numeric AUC improvement claim tied to cDVGAN-generated data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Training the CNN with hybrid samples from our cDVGAN outperforms CNNs trained only on the standard classes, when identifying real samples embedded in LIGO detector background. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: cDVGAN outperforms CNNs trained only on standard classes when identifying real samples embedded in LIGO detector background, achieving a 4% AUC improvement. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: cDVGAN is competitive with traditional data augmentation approaches in a data augmentation example. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This comparative-to-augmentation claim appears in the publication but was not in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The synthetic binary black hole signals generated by cDVGAN are generally consistent with the semianalytical model used for training. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a claim about consistency with semianalytical models that does not appear in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Learned dynamic constraints can be generalized and reused at other time stamps to enhance overall solver performance on temporal problems. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Learned dynamic constraints can be generalized and reused at other time steps, enhancing overall solver performance on temporal problems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion but reports it with lower stated certainty (high -> medium).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A simple translation of the original logic program allows learned constraints to be generalized to other time points. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A general translation is proposed that transforms programs to fulfill the property enabling the generalization of learned constraints. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication formalizes the translation as a general proposal; the contribution is preserved but framed less assertively.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A property of temporal problems exists that enables the generalization of all learned constraints to all time steps. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Most ASP planning encodings either satisfy the property that enables the generalization of learned constraints or can be easily adapted to do so. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication moves from stating the property's existence to emphasizing its prevalence/applicability across encodings, increasing certainty and broadening scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The property that allows generalization of learned constraints is satisfied by many planning problems. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Most ASP planning encodings either satisfy the property that enables the generalization of learned constraints or can be easily adapted to do so. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens and clarifies the prevalence claim, asserting that most encodings satisfy or can be adapted to satisfy the property.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Empirical evaluation shows a positive impact of adding generalized constraints to an ASP solver. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The impact of adding the generalized constraints to an ASP solver has been empirically evaluated. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The empirical-evaluation claim is preserved in the publication with the same substance and confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A key factor in the performance of today’s ASP solvers is conflict-driven constraint learning. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This general contextual conclusion about ASP solvers appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: L-AutoDA automates the design of decision-based adversarial attacks using Large Language Models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: L-AutoDA employs an evolutionary strategy that reduces human intervention in the generation of adversarial attack algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the automation claim by specifying an evolutionary strategy that reduces human intervention (more detail on mechanism) and reduces certainty from high to medium.

alignments.pub_claim_text: L-AutoDA demonstrates substantial superiority over existing baseline methods in terms of success rate and computational efficiency.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings highlight the potential of language models as tools for adversarial attack generation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results highlight the formidable utility of language models in crafting adversarial attacks.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same conclusion but with stronger language about utility, increasing the expressed confidence in the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural Topic Models (NTMs) directly optimize parameters without requiring model-specific derivations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural topic models (NTMs) directly optimize parameters without requiring model-specific derivations.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of attention-boosting gates (AbGs) and attention-boosting modules (AbMs) improves the efficiency of fusing semantic information in the encoder. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.rationale: The numeric performance claim is identical across versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For the extraction and normalization of product attribute values, GPT-4 achieves a similar performance to the extraction scenario. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: For the extraction and normalization of product attribute values, GPT-4 achieves a similar performance to the extraction scenario. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The comparative performance statement is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GPT-4 is particularly strong at string wrangling and name expansion. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GPT-4 is particularly strong at string wrangling and name expansion. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same qualitative strengths are reported in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Web Data Commons - Product Attribute Value Extraction (WDC-PAVE) benchmark dataset consists of product offers from 59 different websites. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The WDC-PAVE benchmark dataset consists of product offers from 59 different websites which provide schema.org annotations. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the qualification that those 59 sites provide schema.org annotations, clarifying and slightly narrowing the dataset description without changing confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cyclic precision training (CPT) achieves impressive improvements in training efficiency while improving DNN performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cyclic precision training achieves particularly impressive improvements in training efficiency while improving DNN performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core claim about CPT's improvements is preserved; publication emphasizes 'particularly' but meaning is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Alternative CPT schedules can offer further improvements in training efficiency and model performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Alternative cyclic precision training schedules offer further improvements in training efficiency and model performance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about alternative schedules mapping to further improvements is the same; publication slightly moderates certainty but retains the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A correlation exists between model performance and training cost, and changing the CPT schedule can control the tradeoff between these two variables. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A correlation exists between model performance and training cost, and changing the underlying cyclic precision training schedule can control the tradeoff between these two variables. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is restated with minor wording ('underlying') but the semantic content is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Aggressive quantization is a form of learning impairment that can permanently damage model performance. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Aggressive quantization is a form of learning impairment that can permanently damage model performance. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The cautionary conclusion about aggressive quantization matches between versions.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Low precision training can significantly reduce the computational overhead of training deep neural networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This general claim about low-precision training appears in the publication but is not present in the preprint, so it is an added publication-level claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ensemble methods, particularly Random Forest and XGBoost, exhibit superior accuracy, precision, and recall compared to other machine learning methods in malware detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ensemble methods, particularly Random Forest and XGBoost, exhibit superior accuracy, precision, and recall compared to other machine learning methods in malware detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication texts are effectively identical in content and strength about ensemble methods' superiority.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Data pre-processing techniques, particularly TF-IDF representation and Principal Component Analysis, significantly improve model performance in malware detection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Data pre-processing techniques, particularly TF-IDF representation and Principal Component Analysis, improve model performance in malware detection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication softens "significantly improve" to "improve," reducing the strength while keeping the same scope and methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study emphasizes the need for continuous adaptation in malware detection to address the evolving nature of malware. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a need for continuous adaptation in malware detection systems to address the evolving nature of malware. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: Both statements convey the same limitation about the need for continuous adaptation against evolving malware with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This research contributes to ongoing discussions in cybersecurity and provides practical insights for developing more robust malware detection systems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study advances cybersecurity capabilities by identifying and mitigating threats more effectively through the evaluation of various classification models. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the general contribution into a more specific claim about advancing threat identification/mitigation via model evaluation, narrowing and toning down the original broader/practical-contribution claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trained neural network predicts the spatially averaged contrast agent concentration for each centerline point of the vasculature over time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model predicts the spatially averaged contrast agent concentration for each centerline point of the vasculature over time, lowering the overall computational demand. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a computational-efficiency benefit to the same predictive capability, broadening the claim while keeping the core result intact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieves a mean absolute error of 0.02 ± 0.02 and a mean absolute percentage error of 5.31% ± 9.25%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The trained network enables the reconstruction of relative contrast agent concentrations with a mean absolute error of 0.02±0.02 and a mean absolute percentage error of 5.31±9.25 %. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numerical performance metrics and interpretation are the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The network is robust to varying degrees of vessel overlap and vessel foreshortening. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The network is robust to varying degrees of vessel overlap and vessel foreshortening. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Wording and claim are identical regarding robustness to overlap and foreshortening.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach demonstrates the potential of the integration of machine learning and blood flow simulations in time-resolved angiographic flow reconstruction. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach demonstrates the potential of the integration of machine learning and blood flow simulations in time-resolved angiographic contrast agent concentration reconstruction. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies "contrast agent concentration reconstruction," clarifying the application while preserving the original claimed potential.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Auxiliary Fourier-basis Augmentation (AFA) is a complementary technique that targets augmentation in the frequency domain. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Auxiliary Fourier-basis Augmentation (AFA) fills the robustness gap left by common visual augmentations. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames AFA's role more explicitly as filling a robustness gap, which clarifies its contribution relative to common augmentations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AFA benefits the robustness of models against common corruptions, out-of-distribution generalization, and consistency of performance against increasing perturbations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AFA benefits the robustness of models against common corruptions, out-of-distribution generalization, and consistency of performance against increasing perturbations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of AFA with other augmentation techniques can further boost performance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AFA can be seamlessly integrated with other augmentation techniques to further boost performance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both texts make the same integrative-conclusion about combining AFA with other augmentations.

alignments.pub_claim_text: AFA has a negligible deficit to the standard performance of models. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This performance-neutrality result appears in the publication but was not stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VTG-GPT significantly outperforms SOTA methods in zero-shot settings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: VTG-GPT significantly outperforms SOTA methods in zero-shot settings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claims are identical in wording and strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VTG-GPT surpasses unsupervised approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: VTG-GPT surpasses unsupervised approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct match with no meaningful change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VTG-GPT achieves competitive performance comparable to supervised methods. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: VTG-GPT achieves competitive performance comparable to supervised methods. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is the same between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our proposed method, HyperPredict, enables the selection of optimal hyperparameters at test time without requiring labelled data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method, HyperPredict, enables the selection of optimal hyperparameters at test time without requiring labelled data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The core capability is stated the same; only minor change in phrasing and typology (contribution vs conclusion).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HyperPredict implements a Multi-Layer Perceptron that predicts the resulting segmentation overlap and measure of deformation smoothness based on selected hyperparameters. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HyperPredict implements a Multi-Layer Perceptron that predicts the resulting segmentation overlap and measures of deformation smoothness based on selected hyperparameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The functional description is the same; the publication labels it as a result rather than a contribution but content is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results demonstrate good performance in predicting the effects of regularization hyperparameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evaluation of HyperPredict on the OASIS brain MR standard benchmark dataset demonstrates good performance in predicting the effects of regularization hyperparameters. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The criteria used to define optimal hyperparameters is flexible post-training, allowing for efficient selection of specific properties. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The criteria used to define optimal hyperparameters in HyperPredict is flexible post-training, allowing for efficient selection of specific properties. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The factors that impact driving behaviors can be summarized into eleven features including speed, acceleration, deceleration, and pedal.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The factors that impact driving behaviors can be summarized into eleven features including speed, acceleration, deceleration, pedal, steering, gear, engine, distance, weather, traffic signal, and road parameters.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands and enumerates additional factors (steering, gear, etc.), providing more detail rather than contradicting the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Supervised/unsupervised learning algorithms and reinforcement learning frameworks have been popularly used to model the vehicle's energy consumption with multi-dimensional data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Supervised/unsupervised learning algorithms and reinforcement learning frameworks have been popularly used to model the vehicle’s energy consumption with multi-dimensional data.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same methodological claim with essentially identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The literature shows that driving data are collected from either simulators or real-world experiments, with real-world data mainly stored and transmitted by various technologies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The literature shows that driving data are collected from either simulators or real-world experiments, with real-world data mainly stored and transmitted by meters, controller area networks, onboard data services, smartphones, and additional sensors installed in the vehicle.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the storage/transmission technologies in detail, expanding the original, more generic statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper recommends nine energy-efficient driving styles based on driving behavior factors, driver characteristics, and safety rules.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper recommends nine energy-efficient driving styles including four guidelines for the drivers’ selection and adjustment of the vehicle parameters, three recommendations for the energy-efficient driving styles in different driving scenarios, and two subjective suggestions for different types of drivers and employers.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the recommendation of nine driving styles but elaborates the components of those recommendations, adding specificity without changing scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We prove exponential emulation rates in Sobolev spaces in terms of the number of neurons and in terms of the number of nonzero coefficients for Gevrey-regular solution classes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We prove exponential emulation rates in Sobolev spaces in terms of the number of neurons and in terms of the number of nonzero coefficients for Gevrey-regular solution classes.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same theoretical result with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Continuous, piecewise polynomial high order finite elements can be exactly emulated by neural networks combining ReLU and ReLU$^2$ activations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Continuous, piecewise polynomial high order finite elements can be exactly emulated by neural networks combining ReLU and ReLU2 activations.
alignments.rationale: The claim is effectively identical (notation change ReLU^2 vs ReLU2 only), so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: On shape-regular, simplicial partitions of polytopal domains, both the number of neurons and the number of nonzero parameters are proportional to the number of degrees of freedom of the finite element space.
alignments.pub_claim_text: On shape-regular, simplicial partitions of polytopal domains, both the number of neurons and the number of nonzero parameters are proportional to the number of degrees of freedom of the hp finite element space.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies 'hp finite element space', making the statement more specific (narrower) than the preprint's generic finite element space phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed data-based approach for modeling in-cylinder pressure and cyclic variation demonstrates an overall accuracy of 13.5% in mean behavior and 65.5% in standard deviation for evaluated combustion measures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed data-based approach successfully models in-cylinder pressure and cycle-to-cycle variations.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the high-level success claim but removes the specific percentage metrics from the preprint, presenting a more general statement of success.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The peak-pressure rise-rate prediction in the proposed model achieves an accuracy of 22.7% in mean behavior and 96.4% in standard deviation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The average prediction error of the mean in-cylinder pressure over a complete combustion cycle is 0.051 bar.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports different numeric metrics (absolute error in bar) for in-cylinder pressure; the correspondence to the preprint's peak-pressure rise-rate percentage metrics is indirect and thus treated as a revision of reported results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Principle Component Decomposition-based approach is an important step towards in-cylinder pressure shaping.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cylinder-pressure-based control enables advanced pre-mixed combustion concepts by allowing for cylinder pressure and heat release shaping.
alignments.rationale: The publication places pressure-shaping in the broader context of enabling advanced combustion concepts, expanding the preprint's methodological emphasis into broader application-level implications.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of Gaussian Process Regression provides important information on cyclic variation and next-cycle controls related to safety and performance criteria.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of Gaussian process regression provides next-cycle control information on safety and performance criteria.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same role for Gaussian process regression in providing next-cycle control information relevant to safety and performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RouteExplainer is a post-hoc explanation framework that explains the influence of each edge in a generated route for the Vehicle Routing Problem.
alignments.pub_claim_text: RouteExplainer is a post-hoc explanation framework that explains the influence of each edge in a generated route for the Vehicle Routing Problem.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution description with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The edge classifier proposed in the framework infers the intentions of each edge and demonstrates rapid computation while maintaining reasonable accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The edge classifier proposed in the framework infers the intentions of each edge and is quantitatively evaluated on four different VRPs.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds quantitative evaluation details (evaluated on four VRPs), giving more concrete evidence for the preprint's claim about the classifier's inference ability and performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The qualitative evaluation of explanations generated by RouteExplainer validates the framework and shows the synergy between explanation frameworks and Large Language Models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Qualitative evaluations of explanations generated by the framework validate its effectiveness and show the synergy between explanation frameworks and Large Language Models.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same qualitative evaluation outcome and notes the synergy with LLMs, consistent with the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our AI models showed high accuracy, with an 88% success rate in identifying texts from individuals with ASD.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of our AI models yielded promising results, with the predictive model reaching an accuracy of almost 88% when classifying texts that potentially originated from individuals with ASD.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports essentially the same accuracy and interpretation of results as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates AI's potential in improving ASD diagnosis, especially in children.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our research demonstrated the potential of using AI, particularly DL models, in enhancing the accuracy of ASD detection and diagnosis.
alignments.rationale: Both versions make the same high-level conclusion about AI's potential to improve ASD detection/diagnosis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The research addresses challenges in traditional ASD diagnosis using machine learning and deep learning techniques.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This innovative approach signifies the critical role AI can play in advancing early diagnostic techniques, enabling better patient outcomes.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the work more broadly as an innovative approach with implications for early diagnostics and patient outcomes, expanding on the preprint's focus on addressing diagnostic challenges.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper establishes a multifaceted definition of salient time steps through extensive need-finding studies with domain experts. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper establishes a multifaceted definition of salient time steps through extensive need-finding studies with domain experts. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel approach leveraging autoencoders and dynamic programming is proposed to facilitate user-driven temporal selections. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel approach leveraging autoencoders and dynamic programming is proposed to facilitate user-driven temporal selections. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The method description and claimed contribution are preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method incorporates structural features, statistical variations, and distance penalties to enable more flexible selections. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method incorporates structural features, statistical variations, and distance penalties to enable more flexible selections. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same technical components and framing of the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A web-based interface is designed and implemented to enable efficient and context-aware selection of time steps. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A web-based interface is designed and implemented to enable efficient and context-aware selection of time steps. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the design and implementation claim with no substantive changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The efficacy and usability of the web-based interface are evaluated through case studies, quantitative evaluations, and expert interviews. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The efficacy and usability of the web-based interface are evaluated through case studies, quantitative evaluations, and expert interviews. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The evaluation components are the same, but the publication presents the evaluation with higher reported certainty (medium → high).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Multiple Kernel k-Means (MKKM) algorithm optimizes base kernel matrices to achieve optimal clustering. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Multiple Kernel k-Means (MKKM) algorithm optimizes base kernel matrices to achieve optimal clustering. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion about MKKM's objective and role.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Relying solely on a single metric to define kernel relationships introduces bias and incomplete characterization, hindering efficient information extraction. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Relying solely on a single metric to define kernel relationships introduces bias and incomplete characterization, hindering efficient information extraction. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel method introduced integrates both kernel correlation and dissimilarity, facilitating more efficient classification information extraction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel method introduced integrates both kernel correlation and dissimilarity, facilitating more efficient classification information extraction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The core methodological contribution is preserved without substantive alteration.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method significantly improves clustering precision by emphasizing the coherence between kernel correlation and dissimilarity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method significantly improves clustering precision and offers a more objective strategy for extracting non-linear information. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the improvement-in-precision claim but adds that the method provides a more objective strategy for extracting non-linear information, broadening the interpreted impact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of our algorithm on 13 challenging benchmark datasets demonstrates its superiority over contemporary state-of-the-art MKKM techniques. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the algorithm is superior to contemporary state-of-the-art MKKM techniques, as assessed on 13 challenging benchmark datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same benchmark-based superiority claim with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed two-stage Dual Contrastive Learning Network improves semi-supervised multi-organ segmentation by utilizing global and local contrastive learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Dual Contrastive Learning Network (DCL-Net) improves semi-supervised multi-organ segmentation by utilizing global and local contrastive learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is restated consistently (naming difference only).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Stage 1 of the method employs similarity-guided global contrastive learning to explore implicit continuity and similarity among images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Stage I of DCL-Net employs similarity-guided global contrastive learning to explore implicit continuity and similarity among images. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Stage 2 introduces organ-aware local contrastive learning to enhance class representations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Stage II of DCL-Net implements organ-aware local contrastive learning to enhance class representations. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A mask center computation algorithm is introduced to reduce the computational burden during local contrastive learning. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A mask center computation algorithm is introduced to compress category representations for local contrastive learning, easing the computation burden. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the mechanism (compressing category representations) while preserving the original intent of reducing computational cost.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments on the 2017 ACDC dataset and an in-house RC-OARs dataset demonstrate the superior performance of the proposed method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments on the 2017 ACDC dataset and an in-house RC-OARs dataset demonstrate the superior performance of the proposed method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Experimental claims and datasets are the same and reported with equal certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The extensive cut-and-paste strategy (ECAP) effectively leverages reliable pseudo-labels through data augmentation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The extensive cut-and-paste strategy (ECAP) effectively leverages reliable pseudo-labels through data augmentation in unsupervised domain adaptation for semantic segmentation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ECAP maintains a memory bank of pseudo-labeled target samples throughout training. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ECAP maintains a memory bank of pseudo-labeled target samples throughout training and cut-and-pastes the most confident ones onto the current training batch. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an operational detail (cut-and-paste of most confident samples) clarifying how the memory bank is used during training.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Implementing ECAP on top of the recent method MIC boosts its performance on two synthetic-to-real domain adaptation benchmarks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Implementing ECAP on top of the recent method MIC boosts its performance on two synthetic-to-real domain adaptation benchmarks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about performance gains when combined with MIC is restated unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MIC+ECAP achieves an unprecedented performance of 69.1 mIoU on the Synthia->Cityscapes benchmark. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MIC+ECAP achieves an unprecedented performance of 69.1 mIoU on the Synthia to Cityscapes benchmark. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric benchmark result is identical and retained in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A GNN model for temperature-dependent CMC prediction of surfactants has been developed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A GNN model has been developed for the temperature-dependent prediction of the critical micelle concentration (CMC) of surfactants. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the development of the same GNN model for temperature-dependent CMC prediction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text:
The model exhibits a high predictive performance of R$^2 
geq 0.94 on test data in both training scenarios. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model exhibits a high predictive performance of R² ≥ 0.95 on test data in both training scenarios. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported numeric performance metric increases from R² ≥ 0.94 to R² ≥ 0.95 in the publication, a revision that amplifies reported accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model performance varies by surfactant class. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the model varies with the surfactant class. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions report the same class-dependent variation in performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sugar-based surfactants represent a more sustainable alternative to synthetic surfactants. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sugar-based surfactants represent a more sustainable alternative to synthetic surfactants and are of great interest for future applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the sustainability claim but extends it by noting interest for future applications, broadening the implication of the conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The policy trained under domain randomization traffic flow has significantly better success rate compared to the models trained under other microscopic traffic flows.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Policies trained with deep reinforcement learning under domain-randomized traffic flow achieve significantly better success rates compared to those trained under non-randomized traffic flow.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same success-rate claim but specifies deep RL and compares to non-randomized flow rather than broadly to other microscopic flows, so the claim is clarified and narrowed in scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The policy trained under domain randomization traffic flow has a calculative reward that is significantly better than that of models trained under other microscopic traffic flows.
alignments.rationale: The published version does not report or restate a claim about a superior calculative reward; that specific result is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current studies on autonomous vehicle decision-making often do not consider the performance degradation when models are tested in realistic traffic scenes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of models trained in simulated environments may degrade when tested in more realistic traffic scenes.
alignments.rationale: The preprint framed this as a limitation of prior work (lack of consideration), while the publication asserts the empirical possibility of degradation — a shift in emphasis (revised) though the topic remains the same.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method randomizes the driving behavior of surrounding vehicles by adjusting parameters of car-following and lane-changing models.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes an explicit methodological description of how domain randomization is implemented which was not stated among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TaylorShift enables computing full token-to-token interactions in linear time and space.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TaylorShift enables computing full token-to-token interactions in linear time and space.
alignments.rationale: The claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TaylorShift enhances memory efficiency for sequences as short as 800 tokens.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All three parts of the method improve the recognizability of generated inks. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: All three parts of the method improve the recognizability of generated inks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same claim about each component improving recognizability but increases the stated certainty from medium to high, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A human study indicates that people perceive most of the generated data as real. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A human study indicates that people perceive most of the generated data as real. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The human-subject perception result is preserved verbatim with the same certainty in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AU intensities are greater for posed than spontaneous datasets, regardless of gender. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AU intensities are greater for posed than spontaneous datasets, regardless of gender. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the claim verbatim with the same certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a smoothing of intensity up to 80 percent for AU6 and 45 percent for AU12 when a real face is transformed into CG. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a smoothing of intensity up to 80% for AU6 and 45% for AU12 when a real face is transformed into CG. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The research aims to evaluate Ekman’s action units in datasets of real human faces, posed and spontaneous, and virtual human faces. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method utilizes minimal available pose labels to learn the underlying 3D geometry of the scene for estimating the 6 DoF camera pose. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method utilizes minimal available pose labels to learn the underlying 3D geometry of the scene for estimating the 6 DoF camera pose. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learning method presented aligns two 3D geometric representations to estimate a pose that matches the pose label. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learning method presented estimates two 3D scene representations in both camera and global coordinate frames. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes estimation of the two representations and the coordinate frames, dropping some detail about alignment to pose labels; the core idea of two representations is preserved but wording is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The formulation allows for the inclusion of additional learning constraints, resulting in improved localization accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The formulation allows for the inclusion of additional learning constraints, which minimizes 3D alignment errors and improves localization accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a specific mechanism (minimizing 3D alignment errors) but preserves the original claim that additional constraints improve localization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model estimates 3D scene geometry in camera and global frames and aligns them rigidly to obtain pose in real-time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learning method presented estimates two 3D scene representations in both camera and global coordinate frames. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the estimation of camera/global representations but omits explicit wording about rigid alignment and real-time operation, narrowing the explicit scope while keeping the main point.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method exceeds state-of-the-art regression methods' pose accuracy on all evaluated visual localization datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model exceeds state-of-the-art regression methods' pose accuracy on three common visual localization datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the SOTA claim but specifies the evaluation on three common datasets rather than 'all evaluated' datasets, narrowing the empirical scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ML models trained on the database obtained using the Bayesian optimization approach consistently outperform the other two databases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ML models trained on the database obtained using the Bayesian optimization approach consistently outperform the other two databases. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: This work contributes to the resource-efficient collection of data in high-dimensional complex parameter spaces. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
